rulings.Case Title: KIM BELLINO VS. UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 19, 2011, line 3, DEFENDANT UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. CONTINUED TO AUGUST 4, 2011. rulings.Case Title: DAVID GRASSI VS. JULIEN SIGNES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Second Amended X-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 19, 2011, line 4, DEFENDANT JULIEN SIGNES Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Cross-Complaint. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM DEWAYNE SCHILLING VS. JAYANT AGARWAL et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 19, 2011, line 5, DEFENDANT JAYANT AGARWAL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED TRIABLE ISSUES OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO BREACH AND CAUSATION. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, A LIMITED VS. DANIEL L MITCHELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel: 1) Further Responses To Deft'S Special Interrogatories [Set One]; 2) Monetary Sanctions In The Sum Of $3040.00
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 19, 2011, line 2, DEFENDANT DANIEL MITCHELL'S Motion To Compel: 1) Further Responses To Defendant's Special Interrogatories [Set One]; 2) Monetary Sanctions In The Sum Of $3040.00. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTEROGATORIES (SET ONE) IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $3040.00 IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS ARE DIRECTED TO PROVIDE RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT BY JULY 29, 2011. PLAINTIFFS ARE FURTHER DIRECTED TO REMIT THE SANCTIONS TO DEFENDANT BY THAT SAME DATE. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: MARK TANNER CONSTRUCTION, INC. et al VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL LTD. et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 19, 2011, line 6, DEFENDANT LOUIS VIGLOTTI'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS BASED ON A LACK OF JURISDICTION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SHOW THAT DEFENDANT HAD THE MINIMUM CONTACTS NECESSARY FOR THIS COURT TO EXERCISE JURISDICTION OVER DEFENDANT. =(302/LMG)
